sources.label: Johnson Controls completes acquisition of Alloy Enterprises - data center liquid cooling (May 13, 2026)
Johnson Controls Q2 FY26 results - record $20B backlog, 40% FX-adjusted order growth, FY26 guidance raised (May 6, 2026)
Barchart via Yahoo Finance: 21-analyst Moderate Buy, mean PT ~$153 (~5% above spot) after post-earnings PT raises

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news in the Jun 6-9 window and a +0.81% drift day; the record-backlog quarter (May 6) and Alloy liquid-cooling close (May 13) were already embedded in yesterday's hold and the 18.7% YTD rerating. Small negative drift on the alpha lens: 21-analyst Moderate Buy with mean PT ~$153 only ~5% above spot means the cooling/controls bottleneck story is increasingly recognized, shrinking residual alpha without changing the thesis.
verdicts.bull: End-to-end data center thermal stack (high-density chillers, Alloy direct liquid cooling, OpenBlue controls) tied to AI power-density growth, with a record $20B backlog and 40% FX-adjusted order growth driven by large-scale data center projects.
verdicts.neutral: Quality compounder executing well, but with consensus targets clustering near spot the easy rerating has happened; returns from here likely track backlog conversion and earnings delivery rather than further multiple expansion.
verdicts.bear: JCI is a diversified buildings company, not a pure bottleneck play - the AI multiple is being applied to a largely conventional business, so any hyperscaler capex digestion headline can compress the rerated multiple disproportionately.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings (late July/early August) showing continued data center order momentum, backlog conversion, and first Alloy liquid-cooling integration traction.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI capex digestion or a hyperscaler cooling-spend pause hitting a crowded 'picks-and-shovels cooling' trade on a stock already priced near consensus targets after a ~19% YTD run.
